title: If a package has the same as the class inside of it then verilator gets confused at using that class in another file (UVM) using import pkg::*
body:
- Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

This repository:
https://github.com/Amirk97/IIR_IP_SystemVerilog
and this commit:
2474b20
run the command make compile_verilator_tb

- What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)
"assExtends 3 | class const_coeff_item extends basic_sequence_item#(); | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ... For error `escription` see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.042"

- What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
verilator -Wno-fatal --binary -j $$(nproc) --top-module top_tb_IIR -CFLAGS "-I./C_code_source_FPGA" \
  +incdir+$$UVM_HOME \
	$$UVM_HOME/uvm_pkg.sv $$FILES

- What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?
Verilator 5.042 2025-11-02 rev `v5.042-1-gd91574507`
Been tried with Xcelium without problem

- What OS and distribution are you using?
Distributor ID: Ubuntu
Description:    Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS
Release:        24.04
Codename:       noble

- May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I may or may not be able to contribute
